question: Is there an official SEP-aligned framework for building a Stellar anchor / on-off ramp?

golden.answer: As of **2026-07-11**, Anchor Platform is SDF's open-source Java/Kotlin platform for building Stellar anchor on/off-ramp services. The canonical repository is `stellar/anchor-platform`; `stellar/java-stellar-anchor-sdk` is a redirected legacy name. Current docs describe support for SEP-1, 6, 10, 12, 24, receive-side 31, 38, and 45. Those modules still require correct configuration, business-server integration, custody/compliance operations, and live testing—adopting the software does not automatically make an operator SEP-compliant. Keep it distinct from the Stellar Disbursement Platform.
keyFacts: Identifies the anchor/on-off-ramp role and canonical current repo.
Dates current SEP coverage and rejects automatic-compliance claims.
avoid: Do NOT conflate Anchor Platform with SDP, a wallet, or an RPC node.
Do NOT call the legacy repo canonical or imply installation alone guarantees conformance.
golden.notes:
Anchor Platform vs SDP confusion is the trap; SEP set is the must_have. Verified: repo is stellar/java-stellar-anchor-sdk; covers SEP-1/6/10/12/24/31/38.
Also good if the answer: Adopting/forking the Anchor Platform inherits SEP conformance rather than re-implementing the protocols. | It also covers further SEPs (e.g. SEP-6 programmatic deposit/withdrawal, SEP-38 quotes).
Golden cites: developers.stellar.org Anchor Platform docs or the stellar/anchor-platform repo.
GT-49 CORRECTION 2026-07-11: grade durable role first; repo/SEP enumeration is dated.

corroboration.claim: Anchor Platform is SDF's open-source on/off-ramp platform; the canonical repo is stellar/anchor-platform and current documented coverage includes SEPs 1,6,10,12,24,31 receive-only,38,45.
